name: Salmon with Fines Herbes Stuffing
recipeInstructions:
1. COMBINE onion, white wine, salt, pepper, and 2 tablespoons butter in a large pot. Place over medium heat and cook for 5 minutes.
2. Add bread crumbs. Cook, stirring constantly, for 3 minutes.
3. Transfer contents to a bowl and cool.
4. Add parsley, tarragon, and thyme.
5. Lay the fillet on the work surface and carefully butterfly it by cutting in half to give you 2 thin fillets.
6. Place parchment paper larger than the 2 fillets side by side on a baking sheet.
7. Brush the parchment with oil.
8. Lay the 2 pieces of salmon on the parchment head to tail, forming a large rectangular fillet of salmon.
9. Spread the cool stuffing evenly over the salmon and roll up the salmon into a cigarette style cylinder.
10. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
11. Place the salmon roll in a baking pan and bake for 35 minutes.
12. Meanwhile, heat the sherry in a saucepan; cook, reducing by half.
13. Remove from heat and whisk in the remaining butter.
14. Unwrap the salmon from the parchment and place on a platter.
15. Pour the sauce over and serve immediately.
